question: Will 2 Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?
notes: {"adapter_venue": "polymarket", "approval": {"by": "approve_trades.py", "status": "approved", "ts_utc": "2026-05-02 19:44 UTC"}, "baseline_components": {}, "category": "macro/fed", "edge_vs_market": 0.05499999999999999, "llm": {"confidence": 0.72, "enabled": true, "model": "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001", "rationale": "As of late April 2026, with ~8 months remaining in the year, 2 cuts would require an aggressive pivot from current Fed policy. question: Will Tottenham be relegated from the English Premier League after the 2025–26 season?
notes: {"adapter_venue": "polymarket", "approval": {"by": "approve_trades.py", "status": "approved", "ts_utc": "2026-05-02 19:44 UTC"}, "baseline_components": {}, "category": "other", "edge_vs_market": -0.3005, "llm": {"confidence": 0.92, "enabled": true, "model": "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001", "rationale": "Tottenham is a top-6 club with strong financial resources, established infrastructure, and managerial stability. Relegation requires catastrophic failure (like administrative points deduction or multiple managers). The 32% crowd price vastly overestimates this tail risk; historical precedent shows no top-6 club has been relegated in the modern Premier League era absent severe sanctions. The crowd is likely mispricing entertainment value or using outdated odds from earlier uncertainty."}, "p_yes_market": 0.3205, "pricing_source": "mid", "snapshot": {"bestAsk": 0.321, "bestBid": 0.32, "id": "572847", "lastTradePrice": 0.32, "liquidity": "22503.76734", "question": "Will Tottenham be relegated from the English Premier League after the 2025\u201326 season?", "slug": "will-tottenham-be-relegated-from-the-english-premier-league-after-the-202526-season", "updatedAt": "2026-04-17T23:07:14.834424Z", "volume": "504400.36135599995"}, "spread": 0.0010000000000000009}

question: Will the Court Force Trump to Refund Tariffs?
